Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed clause with directional noun phrase (rüpü) + motion verb amuley
Figurative Meaning: Someone who lives or acts with integrity and moral clarity
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent: Kiñe che nor rakiduam mew mongen
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, family advice, leadership discourse
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Associated with Mapuche ethical worldview emphasizing balance and correct path in life
First Example Sentence: Chachay nütramkan: Nor rüpü mew amuley tüfa chi pichikeche.
Second Example Sentence: Kiñe longko müley nor rüpü mew amuley tañi lof mew.
Third Example Sentence: Pu che kimniey: nor rüpü mew amuley feychi wentru.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Adverbial phrase with evaluative adjective + motion verb
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent: Küme rakiduam nien ka küme amun
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects Mapuche emphasis on thoughtful deliberation before action
First Example Sentence: Kimeltuchefe piam: küme rakiduam mew amuley tami küdaw.
Second Example Sentence: Pu wenüy kimniey küme rakiduam mew amuley.
Third Example Sentence: Iñche ayün che küme rakiduam mew amuley.

Idiom (Mapudungun): Feychi zugun müley longko rakiduam mew
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Declarative clause with existential verb müley
Figurative Meaning: The decision depends on leadership judgment
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional Mapuche governance structure where the longko guides decisions
First Example Sentence: Trawün mew pu che piam: feychi zugun müley longko rakiduam mew.
Second Example Sentence: Kom che allkütuy, feychi zugun müley longko rakiduam mew.
Third Example Sentence: Lof mew kimniey: feychi zugun müley longko rakiduam mew.

Figurative Meaning: Strength comes from connection to land and heritage
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Deeply tied to Mapuche cosmology and territorial identity
First Example Sentence: Pu kimche nütramkan: mapu mew müley tañi newen.
Second Example Sentence: Feychi wentru kimniey mapu mew müley tañi newen.
Third Example Sentence: Ngillatun mew pu che piam mapu mew müley tañi newen.

Idiom (Mapudungun): Küme zugun amuley pu che mew
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Clause with motion verb metaphorically applied to speech
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects oral tradition and value of respectful speech
First Example Sentence: Kimeltuchefe piam küme zugun amuley pu che mew.
Second Example Sentence: Pu pichikeche allkütuy: küme zugun amuley pu che mew.
Third Example Sentence: Feychi kimün müley küme zugun amuley pu che mew.

Idiom (Mapudungun): Kiñe rüpü müley pu kimün mew
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Existential clause with metaphorical noun rüpü
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional Mapuche teaching metaphors relating learning to journeys
First Example Sentence: Profesor piam: kiñe rüpü müley pu kimün mew.
Second Example Sentence: Pu estudiante kimniey kiñe rüpü müley pu kimün mew.
Third Example Sentence: Tüfachi zugun müley: kiñe rüpü müley pu kimün mew.

Idiom (Mapudungun): Nor che müley nor rüpü mew
Figurative Meaning: An honest person lives on the right path
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent: Nor che küme mongen mew amuley
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional Mapuche sayings about personal integrity
First Example Sentence: Chachay piam: nor che müley nor rüpü mew.
Second Example Sentence: Pu che kimniey nor che müley nor rüpü mew.
Third Example Sentence: Feychi wentru feley: nor che müley nor rüpü mew.

Idiom (Mapudungun): Küme mogen müley küme rakiduam mew
Figurative Meaning: A good life comes from good thinking
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common teaching among elders linking thought and well-being
First Example Sentence: Kimche piam küme mogen müley küme rakiduam mew.
Second Example Sentence: Pu che kimniey küme mogen müley küme rakiduam mew.
Third Example Sentence: Trawün mew piam küme mogen müley küme rakiduam mew.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Clause with metaphorical movement of speech
Figurative Meaning: News travels quickly through the community
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects tight social networks in Mapuche communities
First Example Sentence: Pu wenüy piam zugu amuley mapu mew.
Second Example Sentence: Lof mew kimniey zugu amuley mapu mew.
Third Example Sentence: Feychi zugu amuley mapu mew tüfachi antü.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Existential clause with collective noun phrase
Figurative Meaning: People are thinking or discussing something seriously
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent: Pu che rakiduamün kiñe zugu mew
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects collective decision-making traditions in Mapuche society
First Example Sentence: Trawün mew rakiduam müley pu che mew.
Second Example Sentence: Pu che allkütuy ka rakiduam müley pu che mew.
Third Example Sentence: Feychi zugu mew rakiduam müley pu che mew.
